Best Software

Best Construction Bidding Software (2026)

Win more work, faster. Here's the software that makes your bids sharper and your process tighter.

✓ Updated April 2026

The bidding process is where construction companies win or lose before a shovel ever hits the ground. Underestimate and you lose money. Overestimate and you lose the job. The right bidding software helps you hit the mark — faster, with less rework, and with a professional presentation that builds client confidence.

Here's what the best options look like across different company types and project sizes.

Quick comparison

Software Best For Starting Price Key Strength
Buildertrend Residential builders $499/mo Bid-to-project workflow in one platform
Procore Commercial GCs Custom Subcontractor bid management at scale
Stack Estimators needing takeoff ~$299/mo Fast digital takeoff from plans
Buildxact Small-mid builders $149/mo Integrated takeoff + estimating
JobTread Small GCs $99/mo Simple quote-to-job conversion
Houzz Pro Remodelers $65/mo Proposals + design portfolio

1. Buildertrend — Best for residential builders who want bidding inside their PM system

Price: $499–$1,099/mo (Complete plan for full bidding features)

Buildertrend's Complete plan includes a full bid management suite — you can send bid requests to subs, track their responses, build proposals, convert approved bids into active projects, and sync everything to your job budget. The power is integration: your estimate becomes your budget, which feeds your job cost reports automatically.

For residential builders who want a single platform from first contact through final payment, Buildertrend is the strongest option. The trade-off is price — the Complete plan at $1,099/mo is a real commitment for smaller builders.

Buildertrend — Full bid-to-project workflow for residential builders and remodelers.

Visit Buildertrend →

2. Procore — Best for commercial GC subcontractor bid management

Price: Custom (contact sales)

Procore's bidding module handles the complexity of commercial subcontractor solicitation — managing invite lists, tracking who's bidding and who's passed, collecting leveled bids, and creating award packages. For a commercial GC soliciting 15–30 subs per trade on a large project, a spreadsheet approach becomes unmanageable fast. Procore's bid management keeps it organized.

Procore integrates with Sage, Viewpoint, and other ERPs, making it the choice for mid-to-large commercial GCs who need their bid data to flow into a proper accounting system.

3. Stack — Best dedicated takeoff and estimating tool

Price: ~$299/mo (Pro plan)

Stack is purpose-built for digital takeoff and estimating. You upload your plan sets, measure quantities directly from the drawings (linear feet, square footage, count), and build your estimate from those quantities. It's significantly faster than manual takeoff and much more accurate than eyeballing.

Stack works well as a standalone estimating tool for contractors who already have a PM platform and just need better takeoff capabilities. It integrates with several construction PM platforms.

4. Buildxact — Best for small-to-mid builders who want takeoff + estimating together

Price: $149–$499/mo

Buildxact combines digital takeoff with an estimating database and proposal generation in a single, more affordable package than enterprise options. Australian-founded but widely used in the US, it's particularly popular among small residential builders who find Buildertrend's price prohibitive but need more than a spreadsheet.

The estimating database and supplier pricing integrations help you build accurate estimates faster, and the proposal output is professional enough to win work.

5. JobTread — Best affordable option for smaller GCs

Price: $99–$499/mo

JobTread handles bidding through its estimating and proposal features — you build line-item quotes, present them to clients, collect approvals, and convert accepted bids directly into job budgets. It's not as deep as Buildertrend or Stack for complex estimating, but for contractors running straightforward projects, it covers the essentials at a fraction of the cost.

6. Houzz Pro — Best for remodeling contractors who compete on design

Price: $65–$399/mo

Houzz Pro's proposal tools are built for design-forward remodeling contractors. You can build beautiful, itemized proposals with product selections and design photos embedded directly. Clients review and approve digitally. For contractors who win work through their design aesthetic and client experience, Houzz Pro's proposals are a genuine differentiator.

Houzz Pro — Design-forward proposals that help remodelers win more work at better margins.

Visit Houzz Pro →

What to look for in construction bidding software

  • Takeoff capabilities — Can you measure quantities directly from digital plan sets, or do you need a separate tool?
  • Estimating database — Does it maintain a library of your historical costs and material prices?
  • Sub bid management — Can you solicit bids from subs, track responses, and level competing bids?
  • Proposal output — Is the client-facing proposal professional and easy to customize?
  • Bid-to-budget conversion — Does winning a bid automatically create your job budget, or does it require manual re-entry?
  • Integration with PM platform — Does it talk to your project management and accounting software?

Frequently asked questions

What's the difference between estimating software and bidding software?

Estimating software helps you calculate the cost of a project (takeoff, labor rates, material costs, markup). Bidding software handles the client-facing side — creating proposals, submitting them, tracking whether subs are bidding, and managing the award process. Many platforms do both; some specialize in one or the other.

Do I need separate takeoff software?

Depends on your project complexity. If you're doing simple residential remodeling, an estimating tool with basic measurement features may suffice. If you're estimating commercial projects from full plan sets with multiple trades, a dedicated takeoff tool like Stack or PlanSwift pays for itself quickly.

What does construction bidding software cost?

Expect $99–$499/mo for small business tools. Enterprise platforms like Procore are custom-priced but typically run $500–$2,000+/month depending on volume and modules. Dedicated estimating tools like Stack run around $200–$400/mo.
